# agentic_I2 skeptic-crosscheck · S11-T1-A-E1
from dataclasses import dataclass, field

@dataclass
class ClientRecord:
    client_id: str
    full_name: str
    phones: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)

print(ClientRecord("C001", "Ana Perez", ["999111222"]))

exercises.code:
# agentic_I2 skeptic-crosscheck · S11-T2-B-E1
from dataclasses import dataclass, field

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResolvedEntity:
    entity_id: str
    display_name: str = field(compare=False)

    def __post_init__(self):
        if not self.entity_id.strip():
            raise ValueError("entity_id vacío")

a = ResolvedEntity("E1", "Ana")
b = ResolvedEntity("E1", "Ana actualizada")
c = ResolvedEntity("E2", "Ana")
print(a == b, a == c)
print(len({a, b, c}))

exercises.code:
# agentic_I2 skeptic-crosscheck · S11-T3-A-E2
from dataclasses import dataclass, field

@dataclass
class CaseFile:
    case_id: str
    evidences: list = field(default_factory=list)

    def add_evidence(self, ev: dict) -> None:
        self.evidences.append(ev)

cf = CaseFile("CF1")
cf.add_evidence({"score": 0.1})
cf.add_evidence({"score": 0.2})
print("num=", len(cf.evidences))

selfcheck.chosen_text: Evita el default mutable compartido entre instancias
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Skeptic S11 Q0: contrasté las 4 opciones del selfCheck_stems contra theory/iDo. Descarté distractores que no aparecen como práctica recomendada. Me quedo con índice 2: «Evita el default mutable compartido entre instancias» porque responde «¿Por qué `field(default_factory=list)` y no `= []`?» con el vocabulario del paquete activo (sin mirar correctIndex).
